message.content: I'm getting a foreign key constraint error when creating certain nodes. Error code 787.

content.text:
When binding foreign-key columns from JS/TS to SQLite via Rust, coerce empty strings to NULL — SQLite checks `REFERENCES nodes(uuid)` against `''` and fails with FK constraint error (code 787).

Specifically: `dddd`'s `parent_uuid` is stored as empty string `''`, not `NULL`. On upsert, Rust binds `''` → FK check looks for `nodes(uuid='')` → none → FK fails. Fix: coerce empty strings to `null` before send. Add this coercion anywhere you bind nullable foreign-key fields (parent_uuid, canonical_uuid, route, etc.).

message.content: Now there's a weird bug where my text edits keep reverting mid-keystroke.

content.text:
React `useEffect` that depends on a store-derived object (from `.find()`, `.filter()`, etc.) refires on every store update because `.find()` returns a new reference — depend on the stable key (UUID) instead, or guard the effect body with a key-equality check.

`selectedNode` is `nodes.find(...)` — a new object reference every time `nodes` updates (file watcher, save-triggered rescan). Effect refires → `loadNode` resets `contractText` to stale `node.contract_body` mid-keystroke → edit reverts. Fix: depend on `selectedNode?.uuid` as a stable primitive.

message.content: After restart, the app loses track of which repo was open.

content.text:
Tauri Rust state stored in `Mutex<Option<T>>` is in-memory only and resets to `None` on full process restart — don't rely on it alone for anything that must survive restart. Pair it with a durable source (localStorage, SQLite, or a config file) and rehydrate on boot.

Rust `RepoState` is in-memory only (`Mutex<Option<PathBuf>>`). After full restart, it's `None` — but nodes still render from SQLite cache. `getRepoPath()` returns null → store stays null → save skipped. Fix: persist repo path in `localStorage` on open, rehydrate on boot.

message.content: I keep having to tell you about Rust changes but you say it should work already.

content.text:
Tauri TS changes hot-reload instantly; Rust changes (new commands, signature changes, FK binding logic, handler registration) require Ctrl+C and `npm run tauri dev` restart. When shipping a cross-boundary fix, explicitly tell the user which side needs a restart.

TS + Rust both green. Tauri will hot-reload TS; Rust changes require a restart of the dev server (Ctrl+C then `npm run tauri dev` again) for `upsert_node_pub` to use the new `parent_fk` binding. I should always flag this going forward.

message.content: The Code tab is throwing ENOENT on a file that definitely exists.

content.text:
When the Inspector Code tab fails with ENOENT on `fs::canonicalize(repo_path.join(rel_path))`, the primary suspect is a repo/contract mismatch (contract was seeded in repo A but repo B is loaded) — not a missing file. Always verify `getRepoPath()` + contract provenance before touching the resolve logic.

The Code tab isn't broken — it's a repo/contract mismatch. The node has `code_ranges[0].file: src/components/CheckoutButton.tsx`. That file exists at `/tmp/phase6-uat/src/components/CheckoutButton.tsx`. The loaded repo in the running app isn't `/tmp/phase6-uat` — hence canonicalize → ENOENT.

message.content: I clicked the Reconcile button and nothing happened. Is it working?

content.text:
User-visible actions (button clicks, command triggers) must never reduce to a silent `console.log`. Emit inline feedback (toast, amber hint, status line) even when the underlying feature is stubbed — otherwise the user can't distinguish 'clicked and nothing happened' from 'clicked and it silently worked'.

Reconcile button — visible inline 'Reconcile flow ships in Phase 7' hint instead of silent `console.log`. I've updated this to show the amber hint so you get clear feedback.

message.content: For debugging the running app, I keep having to paste console output. Is there a better way?

content.text:
When an agent needs to debug a running Tauri/desktop app without direct UI access, wire a `devlog` Rust command + `installDevLog()` TS shim that mirrors `console.*` to `/tmp/<app>.log` — the agent can then tail the file directly instead of asking the user to copy-paste console output repeatedly.

Add a tiny hook in `main.tsx` that mirrors `console.*` to a file the Tauri side writes. Then I `tail -f /tmp/contract-ide.log` via Bash. You keep the app running; I see every log I asked you to paste. ~20 lines of code. This eliminates the copy-paste loop entirely for future debugging sessions.
